products.name: Classic Himalayan Pink Salt Makhana
products.description: Slow-roasted to crispy perfection and lightly dusted with mineral-rich Himalayan pink salt and a touch of organic olive oil. The perfect clean, guilt-free daily snack.

products.name: Tangy Tomato Herbs Makhana
products.description: A delightful blend of sun-dried tomato powder, dynamic Indian spices, and aromatic Italian herbs. It is sweet, sour, and intensely flavorful.

products.name: Smoked Peri-Peri Fire Makhana
products.description: For those who love a spicy kick! Coated with a fiery seasoning of African bird's eye chilies, garlic, lemon peel, and smoked paprika.

products.name: Gourmet White Cheddar Makhana
products.description: Indulge in the cheesy goodness! Roasted fox nuts seasoned with real, premium aged white cheddar cheese for a smooth, melt-in-your-mouth flavor.

products.name: Golden Butter Caramel Makhana
products.description: A sweet sensation. Glazed with a crispy coating of artisanal melted brown sugar, butter, and a pinch of sea salt. Sweet snacking done right.

products.name: Sour Cream & Onion Zest Makhana
products.description: A classic savory combo. Crispy fox nuts tossed in smooth, tangy sour cream seasoning and garnished with fine chives and wild spring onion flavor.

recipes.name: Royal Creamy Makhana Kheer
recipes.description: A luxurious and healthy twist to the traditional Indian rice pudding. Made with slow-reduced low-fat milk, roasted ground makhana, saffron, cardamom, and garnished with pistachios.
instructions: Lightly crush 3/4 of the roasted makhana, keeping the remaining 1/4 whole.
Bring the milk to a boil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add saffron strands and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes until it slightly thickens.
Add both crushed and whole makhana to the milk. Cook on medium-low heat for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the makhana becomes soft and absorbs the milk flavor.
Stir in the cardamom powder and sliced nuts. Cook for 2 more minutes.
Turn off the heat. Let it cool slightly, then stir in the jaggery powder or sweetener of choice. (Adding jaggery to boiling milk can curdle it).
Serve warm or chilled, garnished with extra saffron and nuts.

recipes.name: Tangy Sprouts & Makhana Chaat
recipes.description: An incredibly fresh, crunchy, and oil-free high-protein salad/chaat. Packed with sprouts, juicy cucumbers, tomatoes, seasoned with zesty lemon juice and mint chutney.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the boiled moong sprouts, chopped onions, tomatoes, and cucumbers.
Drizzle the mint-coriander chutney and fresh lemon juice over the mixture.
Sprinkle the chaat masala and salt according to taste. Toss everything gently to mix.
Just before serving, add the crispy roasted makhana and mix quickly so the makhana stays crunchy.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves, pomegranate seeds, and a pinch of black salt. Enjoy immediately!

recipes.name: Mughlai Makhana Peas Curry
recipes.description: A rich, creamy, and flavorful main course curry. Lightly roasted makhana and tender green peas simmered in a velvet tomato, cashew, and aromatic onion-based gravy.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a pan and sauté the ginger-garlic paste and onion paste until golden brown.
Add tomato puree and cook until the oil starts separating from the masala.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and cashew paste. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring continuously.
Pour in 1.5 cups of warm water to form a smooth gravy. Bring it to a gentle boil.
Add the boiled green peas and roasted makhana. Simmer covered for 5-7 minutes so the makhana absorbs the rich gravy.
Stir in garam masala and heavy cream (optional). Garnish with fresh coriander and serve warm with Roti or Naan.
